messages.text: I have been looking at real estate options and the supply of existing commercial options is very limited. I have also engaged experts in the broader commercial real estate industry and they believe the commercial real estate market is over leveraged and will likely take a big hit in 12 months. A lot of loans will be needing renewal and the higher interest rates are going to contribute to the big hit. On the flip side, land prices have stabilized and are expected to be stable for the next 2-3 years. Bringing all this together, a sound strategy could be pursuing agricultural land in a strategic local that could be rezoned to commercial and move forward with building once the commercial market dives. I will look at agricultural property options that could be good options.
The agricultural property can be farmed and cover taxes, insurance and probably the interest costs on a loan.
I like that idea...there are numerous farm tax advantages...
Here are 3 industrial lots in Ames. Normally, 30% down is needed for a commercial loan. We can get agricultural land for less than commercial/industrial.

The way I have done things before like other developers, if we select agricultural land that has at least 6feet or more of good topsoil, we can strip it for sale and cover most if not all of the cost of the water/sewer and driveway. It also cuts the cost of the building foundation since the equipment is already onsite.
